title: Remove IPGT token error, make document upload optional on IP registration

requirements.summary: Remove all token balance checks and related error messages from the IP registration form
acceptanceCriteria: No 'Insufficient IPGT tokens' or equivalent token balance error message appears anywhere on the registration form or after submission
The form does not perform any token balance check before or during submission
All other form validation (title, category, jurisdiction) remains intact

file_operations.description: Remove all token balance checks, 'Insufficient IPGT tokens' error states, and any conditional logic that blocks or warns users about insufficient token balance. Strip out any useQuery or hook calls that fetch token balance for validation purposes. Ensure the registration form can submit without token-related validation while preserving all other form validation (title, category, jurisdiction, document hash).

requirements.summary: Make the file upload input optional on the IP registration form
acceptanceCriteria: The file upload input does not have a 'required' attribute or equivalent validation constraint
Submitting the registration form without uploading a file does not produce a validation error
If a file is uploaded, the SHA-256 hash is still computed and populates the document hash field as before
If no file is uploaded and no manual hash is provided, the document hash field may remain empty and the form still submits successfully

file_operations.description: Remove any 'required' constraint, validation error, or submission guard related to the file upload input. Allow the form to submit successfully without an attached document. If a file is uploaded, preserve the existing SHA-256 hash computation and auto-population of the document hash field. If no file is uploaded and the document hash field is empty, the form must still submit without validation errors.
